title: frontend: units: Fix parseRam not handling milli-bytes (m) suffix
author: yu-heejin

findings.description: Using `parseInt(value, 10)` before dividing by 1000 is safe for the values seen in practice (up to ~1.47e13, well within JS safe-integer range). A brief comment noting this is fine for Kubernetes resource quantities would help future maintainers.
The early-return guard `/^\d+m$/.test(value)` runs before the general regex, preventing any ambiguity with the megabyte `M` suffix (separate regex branch).
